[   0.5857 ] ERROR: might be timeout in USB write.
ERROR: carveout /misc/carveout/aux_info@CARVEOUT_UNUSED1/ is not supported
ERROR: /misc/tsc_controls/tsc_locking_config is not supported
Error: Reading board information failed.

expected.expected_fix_summary: Known JetPack 5.1.1/5.1.2 issue on AGX Orin: apply the PCN210100 overlay patch from Jetson Linux r35.3.1 / r35.4.1 (see AGX Orin FAQ, USB-timeout section).

input:
Error: No space left on device
Flash failed: APP partition 14GB is too small for the installation
Using flash_l4t_t194_nvme.xml layout

expected.expected_fix_summary: SDK Manager defaults a 14GB APP partition on T194 NVMe flow. Run nvsdkmanager_flash.sh directly with flash_l4t_external.xml (instead of flash_l4t_t194_nvme.xml) so the external-storage partition layout is used and the APP partition fills NVMe.

11:42:59.515 - Error: *** ERROR: flashing failed.
11:42:59.519 - Error: [exec_command]: /bin/bash -c /home/<user>/.nvsdkm/replays/scripts/JetPack_6.2_Linux/NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP.sh; [error]: *** ERROR: flashing failed.
11:42:59.519 - Info: [ Component Install Finished with Error ]
Failure root cause: Failed Component ID: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_AGX_ORIN_TARGETS#36.4. Error Code: 1011. Error: Failed to execute commands with GenericInstaller.

expected.expected_fix_summary: kernel/Image is corrupted on the host filesystem (not a valid gzip archive). Delete the downloaded JetPack files and re-download, or switch to a different host computer (OP confirmed switching machines resolved the issue, suggesting host-side filesystem corruption rather than network/firewall).

[multiple retries 12:13:40, 12:16:12 — same error]
12:16:12.995 - error: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP: [exec_command]: bash -c /home/<user>/.nvsdkm/replays/.../NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP.sh; [error]: <truncated>
Failure root cause: Failed Component ID: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_ORIN_NANO_8GB_DEVKIT#36.4. Error Code: 1011.
- NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_ORIN_NANO_8GB_DEVKIT: Failed - 00:04:05
********************** Completed install for bundle JetPack 6.1 Linux for Jetson Orin Nano [8GB developer kit version] time: 03-Jan-2025-12.16:13 is succeeded: false **********************
[host detected: WSL on Windows 10]

expected.expected_fix_summary: On WSL the kernel/Image fails to stream over USB to the recovery-mode Jetson, surfacing as 'not in gzip format'. WSL does not support flashing external storage devices (documented in SDK Manager Known Issues). Switch to a native Ubuntu host for Orin Nano flashing.

input:
12:54:45.283 - info: Component install GA report: Component Install: NV_L4T_FILE_SYSTEM_AND_OS_COMP#36.4 - finished failed Error Code: 1011. Error: Failed to execute commands with GenericInstaller.
Failure root cause: Failed Component ID: NV_L4T_FILE_SYSTEM_AND_OS_COMP@JETSON_ORIN_NANO_TARGETS#36.4. Error Code: 1011. Error: Failed to execute commands with GenericInstaller.
[multiple dependent components: 500 'Depends on failed component' — TENSORRT, CUDA, CUDNN, OPENCV, ...]
12:54:45.383 - error: completeSetup failed. Error:
12:54:45.383 - error: install process failure

expected.expected_fix_summary: NV_L4T_FILE_SYSTEM_AND_OS_COMP failed during filesystem flash on Orin Nano. SDK Manager's GenericInstaller wraps the flash.sh invocation and the failure is opaque. Recommended path: download the Driver Package (BSP) + Sample Root Filesystem from the Jetson Linux r36.4.3 download page and flash manually via Linux_for_Tegra/flash.sh, following the Quick Start guide. This bypasses SDK Manager's filesystem step.

16:14:29.052 - error: [   0.0562 ] ERROR: failed to read rcm_state
16:14:36.059 - error: command error code: 1
16:14:42.473 - error: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_AGX_ORIN_TARGETS: [   0.0252 ] ERROR: failed to read rcm_state
16:14:42.473 - error: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_AGX_ORIN_TARGETS: Error: Invalid target board - holoscan-devkit.
16:14:42.473 - error: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_AGX_ORIN_TARGETS: *** ERROR: flashing failed.
16:14:42.473 - error: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_AGX_ORIN_TARGETS: [exec_command]: /bin/bash -c /tmp/tmp_NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP.<user>.sh; [error]: *** ERROR: flashing failed.
16:14:42.497 - error: command error code: 11
16:14:42.514 - error: Session first failure occurred while Installing. Root cause component: NV_L4T_FLASH_JETSON_LINUX_COMP@JETSON_AGX_ORIN_TARGETS. Root cause error code: (1011: Failed to execute commands with GenericInstaller)
********************** Completed install for bundle JetPack 6.1 Linux for Jetson AGX Orin modules time: 30-Sep-2024-16.17:50 is succeeded: false **********************

expected.expected_fix_summary: The log explicitly shows 'Invalid target board - holoscan-devkit' followed by 'failed to read rcm_state' — the user has a Holoscan Devkit (which is based on AGX Orin module hardware) but the flash flow does not recognize it as a valid AGX Orin module target. Either (a) the user actually has a standard AGX Orin Developer Kit and the wrong SDK Manager target was selected — re-run SDK Manager and select the correct board, or (b) the user has a Holoscan Devkit and needs the Holoscan-specific flash flow rather than the standard Jetson SDK Manager flow.

input:
[Log archive: SDKM_logs_JetPack_6.2.2_Linux_for_Jetson_AGX_Orin_modules_2026-04-10_10-51-27.zip]
[Target board parsed from filename: JETSON_AGX_ORIN_MODULES (the SoM-only variant used by Holoscan / custom carriers, not the standard Dev Kit)]
[JetPack: 6.2.2]
10:34:37.225 - error: Cannot get user selected groups
10:50:37.214 - error: Failed to send GA4 event. Error: RequestError: read ETIMEDOUT  [telemetry network noise, not actionable]
10:52:38.080 - info: Setting custom dimension value EventLabel - Error Code: 0. Error: . Component ID: JETSON_AGX_ORIN_TARGETS DeviceConnectError: . RetriesCount: 0. UserRetryClicks: 0.
[No 'Failure root cause' line in the log — install ran without standard component errors, but OP reported MCU firmware flash did not succeed]

expected.expected_fix_summary: MCU firmware flashing on AGX Orin modules (not Dev Kit) requires selecting 'USB' as the storage device in SDK Manager's dropdown before initiating the flash — otherwise SDK Manager targets the default eMMC and the MCU firmware path is not exercised, leaving no error trace in the install log.

input:
Flashing stuck at 99%, installation stuck at 99.8%
[Post-boot, multiple services fail to start: gdm, xrdp, nomachine, containerd, colord]
[1019.8588] tegrarcm_v2 --ismb2
[OP host detected: VirtualBox VM, directory 'vboxuser']

expected.expected_fix_summary: Flashing from a VM (VirtualBox / VMware) is unsupported by SDK Manager — USB passthrough to the recovery-mode Jetson is unreliable and surfaces as a hang at the final 99% step. Switch to a native Ubuntu host. If the native flow also fails, capture UART serial logs from the Jetson for further diagnosis.
